channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump’s Asia return and East Timor’s entry take spotlight in landmark ASEAN summit
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Southeast Asian foreign ministers opened talks Saturday ahead of a landmark ASEAN summit that will formally welcome East Timor as the bloc’s 11th member and mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump urged his supporters in New Jersey late Friday to cast a ballot for the Republican candidate for governor, Jack Ciattarelli, before early voting starts in the state on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Filipino social media star dies at 19 in her Los Angeles home, family says
item.description: Emman was the daughter of Filipino television presenter Alejandro "Kim" Ilagan Atienza, also known as Kuya Kim, and his wife Felicia Hung Atienza, a celebrity entrepreneur and health and well-being advocate.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University during homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, WGN 9

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Holmes Norton scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: 3 convicted for illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite
item.description: Three people have been convicted of illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite National Park in recent weeks, the park announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: John Houghton, KREX 5

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York adds $11M to hunger programs amid SNAP cuts, government shutdown
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced $11 million in state funding for emergency food relief programs and urged the USDA to release $700 million in SNAP benefits to prevent a public health crisis, while the Fiscal Policy Institute suggested the state use its budget surplus to backfill the lost benefits.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: Over 141,000 bottles of cholesterol medication have been recalled due to “failed dissolution specifications,” suggesting that the drugs did not pass a solubility test, or failed to dissolve at a standard rate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Dashcam video shows deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant trucker
item.description: Federal officials warned California about truck license compliance issues before deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant driver killed three.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of curling irons recalled amid burn injuries
item.description: Thousands of curling irons sold at popular retailers, including on Amazon, are being recalled after reports of the curling iron barrel detaching, posing a burn risk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump to meet with Asian leaders
item.description: President Donald Trump is going to Japan and South Korea next week to promote an epic financial windfall — at least $900 billion in investments for U.S. factories, a natural gas pipeline and other projects.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Police shoot at truck outside Coast Guard base during immigration crackdown protests in California
item.description: Law enforcement officers shot at a truck outside of a U.S. Coast Guard base in Alameda, California, late Thursday night as protests over the Trump administration's immigration agenda rage on.
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026
item.description: The Social Security cost-of-living increase will go into effect for Social Security recipients beginning in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, KRON4

item.title: Son learns fate years after brutal family murders in wealthy coastal enclave
item.description: Camden Burton Nicholson faces life in prison after his conviction for murdering his parents and housekeeper, and a sanity phase trial will determine his sentence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As federal agents ramp up Chicago immigration crackdown, more elected officials caught in crosshairs
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hoan Huynh was going door-to-door informing businesses of ramped up immigration enforcement on Chicago’s North Side when the Democratic state lawmaker got an activist notification of federal agents nearby.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How fast is your food? Study reveals the speediest drive-thrus for 2025
item.description: From coffee to prescription drugs, Americans love making a purchase from the comfort of their vehicle – but when it comes to speed of service, not all drive-thrus are equal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Here's where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Couple accused of moving, leaving child behind while he was at school
item.description: A 12-year-old Texas boy came home from school to find it empty and his family gone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA is reopening some 2,100 offices to help farmers access $3B in aid despite the ongoing shutdown
item.description: The Agriculture Department will reopen about 2,100 county offices all across the country Thursday despite the ongoing government shutdown to help farmers and ranchers get access to $3 billion of aid from existing programs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: A United Airlines emergency landing likely caused by collision with a weather balloon
item.description: A United Airlines plane appears to have collided with a weather balloon while cruising over Utah at 36,000 feet last week, fracturing layers of its windshield and forcing an emergency landing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Francisco leaders push back against Trump's National Guard threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Donald Trump keeps threatening to send National Guard troops to San Francisco next, blasting the California city as crime-ridden and saying its residents are clamoring for federal help.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Senate Republicans gathering at White House as shutdown effects worsen
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans arrived at the White House on Tuesday — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5
